description.value: Cloud-native distributed SQL database providing PostgreSQL-compatible, strongly consistent, horizontally scalable transactional storage for high-availability applications.
description.comment: Self-described as a cloud-native, distributed SQL database designed for high availability and effortless scale.

license.comment: As of v24.3 (Nov 2024) released under the source-available CockroachDB Software License (CSL); earlier versions were BSL 1.1 and originally Apache 2.0. No defined tag for CSL/source-available; 'proprietary' is the closest fit since the current license is not OSI open-source (it is free for individuals and small businesses but restricts use). Checked 2026-06-09.

first-release.comment: Cockroach Labs founded 2015; first public beta of CockroachDB released in 2015.

latest-version.comment: Latest stable release shown in the official docs version selector as of 2026-06-09.

actively-maintained.comment: Repository pushed 2026-06-09; quarterly release cadence with v26.2.x current.

database-type.comment: Primarily a distributed relational (SQL) DBMS; DB-Engines classifies it as Multi-Model (relational plus JSON/document, spatial, vector).

query-language.comment: PostgreSQL-compatible SQL dialect, wire-compatible with the PostgreSQL protocol.

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID, serializable distributed transactions by default on top of a Raft-replicated key-value store.

schema-model.comment: Schema-enforced relational tables; JSONB columns allow schemaless data within an enforced schema.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Native: data is automatically range-partitioned and distributed across nodes; no external sharding required.

replication.comment: Raft-based synchronous replication (3 replicas by default); every node can serve reads and writes for its ranges (multi-active availability), so there is no single primary.

clustering.comment: Built-in clustering is the core architecture; nodes form a single distributed cluster without third-party tools.

cloud-offering.comment: CockroachDB Cloud is an official fully-managed service (Basic/serverless, Standard, Advanced plans).

full-text-search.comment: Built-in full-text indexes with TSVECTOR/TSQUERY and multiple language dictionaries (PostgreSQL-style).

json-support.comment: JSONB data type with inverted (GIN) index support for querying schemaless data; relational DB, not a document store.

geospatial.comment: Native spatial data types, spatial indexes and PostGIS-compatible functions (not the full PostGIS function set).

time-series.comment: No specialized native time-series engine; time-series data is handled via standard relational tables, not a dedicated time-series feature.

stored-procedures.comment: Supports stored procedures (SQL and PL/pgSQL); some PostgreSQL feature gaps remain.

triggers.comment: Supports triggers with documented limitations relative to PostgreSQL.

views.comment: Standard views and materialized views supported.

official-gui.comment: Built-in web DB Console served by each node (default http://localhost:8080) for monitoring and administration.

cli-tool.comment: Official 'cockroach' command-line binary handles node startup, SQL shell, and cluster operations.

docker-image.comment: Vendor-published official image 'cockroachdb/cockroach' on Docker Hub, regularly updated. Not part of the Docker Official Images (library) program but is the official publisher image.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow tag pages were not fetchable in this environment, so the exact 'cockroachdb' tag question count could not be reliably verified. Left null rather than guess.

db-engines-rank.comment: Overall DB-Engines rank #71 (score 4.16); ranks #1 among Multi-Model DBMS. Checked 2026-06-09; rankings change monthly.

learning-curve.comment: Uses familiar PostgreSQL-compatible SQL with strong documentation (eases learning), but distributed-systems concepts (ranges, replication, multi-region, consistency tuning) add operational complexity. Mid-range assessment.
